title: Global citizenship and sustainable development education
subtitle: An index from 0 (worst) to 1 (best) measuring the extent to which global citizenship and sustainable development education are mainstreamed into different strands of education: national education policies, student assessment, teacher education, and education curricula.
caption: Four countries in 2020 report index scores measuring the mainstreaming of global citizenship and sustainable development education across four strands: national education policies, student assessment, teacher education, and education curricula. The index runs from 0 (worst) to 1 (best), and the bars show values for each strand within each country. Colombia and India both record the maximum score of 1 in national education policies and student assessment; Colombia’s education curricula and teacher education sit at 0.88 and 0.85, while India’s teacher education and education curricula are at 0.95 and 0.92. Malawi also reaches 1 in national education policies and student assessment, with education curricula at 0.91 and teacher education at 0.90. Bulgaria’s scores are lower across all strands: teacher education at 0.73, student assessment at 0.71, education curricula at 0.65, and national education policies at 0.56. Within each country, the bars cluster near the top of the scale for Colombia, India, and Malawi, with the smallest gaps between strands; Bulgaria’s bars span a wider range, with the lowest value in national education policies and the highest in teacher education. Across the four countries, the highest visible values are the 1s recorded by Colombia, India, and Malawi in two strands each, and the lowest visible value is Bulgaria’s 0.56 in national education policies.
